So to recap what we have so far discussed.
First thing we talked about the main thing, the main thing is the soul.
In human form of life, we have to invest time and energy in nourishing the soul, take care
of our spiritual upliftment, our spiritual development and aim for God-realization and
self-realization.
The second point we discussed was nectar and poison.
Pottery apparently is poison in the beginning but it is nectar in the end.
So we should be wise in pursuing happiness.
The third point is understand the signs of karma.
I repeat, understand the signs of karma.
We all are wanting to be happy but let us have a clear understanding of karma.
Why somebody is happy, why somebody is miserable?
Krishna is saying I am equal to everyone.
Somebody is so miserable, so miserable, even if you want to help him, you can't do anything.
Why?
On one side, the Lord is said to be all kind, all merciful, he is well wisher of everyone
but we see somebody is so miserable, such a pathetic condition.
Even if you want to help him, you can't help him, you are helpless.
So we should understand the signs of karma.
Our scriptures have given a wonderful understanding why somebody is suffering, why somebody is
so-called having a good time.
Karma fructifies, there are three stages of karma.
Kutam, Bijam and Falon Mukham.
I repeat, Kutam, Bijam and Falon Mukham.
When we act, whatever action it may be, except for devotional service, an act of serving
the Lord, all our so-called actions, even pious and impious actions, have a certain
reaction.
And the reaction gets stored in the form of Bijam.
Bijam means seed.
Just like when you have a seed, it takes time for the seed to sprout, for it to germinate,
for it to become a small sapling and then it become a plant, then it grows, then flowers
and then finally there is fruit, Falon Mukham.
That's why.
There's a time lag between our action, good or bad, which gets deposited as a Bijam and
which actually manifests as happiness or suffering in our life.
There's a time lag.
And the time gap can be, it can be practically instantaneous, it can be, you know, you act
and you get a reaction.
It can be after a minute.
It can be after one hour.
It can be after one day.
It can be after one year.
It can be after one lifetime.
It can be after many, many lifetimes.
And that's why people are bewildered.
He's such a nice man, but why is he suffering?
He is such a wicked person, such a cruel person.
He is doing all kinds of nonsense, but apparently he's enjoying.
The law of karma is very stringent.
Whatever we do, there is a reaction.
But reaction is suffering.
Reaction is stored in the form of Bijam.
Bijam to translate into Falon Mukham takes time.
And because of that, unless we have Shastra Chakshu, we'll be bewildered.
We'll even wonder where is God.
So what is, we understand Bijam.
Bijam is a seed, the reaction which is dormant, which is waiting for Kala, under the influence
of Kala.
And that's when we actually enjoy or suffer the reactions.
Whatever state we are in currently today, enjoying or suffering, having a pleasant experience
or the unpleasant experience in a very congenial atmosphere or hostile environment, it is because
of our doings in the past.
So in that sense, we should not blame anybody.
Yes.
Others can be an immediate cause.
But we should not blame anybody.
We should not blame anybody.
We should not blame our own cause.
But the real cause is we ourselves.
A devotee understands that and because of which he does not play the blame game.
He understands that whatever situation I am in, it's because of my doing.
So Bijam is a seed and Falon Mukham is when that seed manifests in our life as suffering
or enjoyment.

That happens. So, when we engage in Bhakti or devotional service, in the acts of devotional service, Shravanam, Kirtanam, Vishnu Smaranam, not only it burns the reactions of the deeds we have done in the past, our scriptures say just like you take seeds, you take seeds and you put them on a fire.

You take a fine pan, put them on fire and roast it. The seeds get roasted. Now, you take that seed and put it in a soil with nice manure, with nice moisture and all that. It will not sprout. It has been burned. That seed has lost its potency.
So, likewise, all the sinful reactions which will manifest in our life in the form of suffering, those seeds of the reactions, of the actions which we have done unknowingly, unintentionally,

in the past, even the sinful activities, unintentionally, out of ignorance, which are deposited in our system as bijam, which will sprout in our life in due course of time, those bijams get burned to ashes when we engage in activities of devotional service.
But we should not take Bhakti as an act where we continue doing sinful activities and burn the reactions by engaging in activities of devotional service.

In that case, it will not act. One of the offences to the holy name is to commit sinful activities on the strength of the holy name, knowing that holy name has a potency to burn to ashes the reactions of our past deed.
Whatever we have done in the past, whatever bijams are stored in our system will be burned when we engage in activities of devotional service.

be wrong in our judgment. We rather focus on improving ourselves. What profit the man

of our soul. Janmasartagakari karopaka. Last question from Chandrakant Chaudhary. How to
compensate the shortfall of number of rounds, 16 rounds daily due to some unavoidable circumstances?
Yes, if you have taken a commitment to chant 16 rounds, not only 16 rounds, whatever a commitment,
if for some reason on that particular day we miss out, we do it the next day. But we should not
keep piling up, okay, two rounds of today, two rounds of tomorrow and then we have totally of
18 rounds pending. That should not happen. If you are not able to do today, try to catch up tomorrow.
And that's how we can catch up in case we are not well or whatever reason, we're not able to
complete our spiritual commitment on that day. We can catch up the next day. Thank you very much.
